Title: Targeted plant hologenome editing for plant trait enhancement

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; eto1 background in complementation context
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ACC degradation branch affecting ethylene precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant growth and development and influences nutrient uptake, including micronutrient uptake and P-related responses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoot aerial parts measured in this study
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: ACC breakdown products; lowers available ACC pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Degrades ACC, thereby reducing the plant ACC precursor pool available for conversion into ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Side-route competing with ethylene formation from ACC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting for microbial metabolism; key control point for host ethylene precursor availability in the holobiont context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Bacterial knockout mutant (AcdS-); plant inoculation; rescue/complementation by WT bacteria; ethylene measurement; shoot nutrient profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of acdS increases plant ethylene production and raises shoot Fe, Zn, Cu, and P concentrations; WT acdS+ bacteria suppress the eto1 high-ethylene phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deactivation of bacterial acdS caused a 2.45-fold increase in shoot ethylene in Arabidopsis compared with WT bacteria. 2. acdS loss increased shoot micronutrients including Fe, Zn and Cu, and also increased P. 3. WT ACC deaminase-producing bacteria rescued the high-ethylene eto1 phenotype, showing microbial control of the host ACC pool.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ACC; plant ETO1/ACS5 module (functional interaction at network level)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or selecting microbiomes with reduced ACC deaminase activity could increase shoot micronutrient content; could also be used oppositely to buffer excessive ethylene phenotypes depending on deployment context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats acdS as a microbial functional homolog/antagonist of plant ETO1 in a shared regulatory network rather than as a plant gene; bacterial colonization density was similar between WT and mutant, excluding fitness differences as the main cause
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Gnotobiotic/root inoculation system; modified Hoagland conditions; controlled growth chamber conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: WT acdS+ bacteria reduced shoot ethylene and rescued eto1 phenotype; broader field-performance tradeoffs not tested

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Negative post-transcriptional regulator of ACC synthase 5
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant growth and development and modulates nutrient uptake and nutrient-related traits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoot aerial parts measured in this study
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ethylene biosynthesis via control of ACC synthase activity and ACC pool size
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates ACS5 activity post-transcriptionally, thereby limiting ACC synthesis and ethylene production
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Environmental inputs influence plant ACS-generated ACC pool; specific upstream signals for ETO1 not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key negative controller of host ethylene production; its loss strongly elevates ethylene and nutrient accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Plant knockout mutant (eto1); ethylene measurement; shoot nutrient profiling; microbial rescue with WT acdS+ bacteria
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ETO1 loss increases shoot ethylene and elevates shoot Fe, Zn, Cu, and P concentrations
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. eto1 mutation caused a 2.2-fold increase in shoot ethylene relative to Col-0. 2. ETO1 loss increased shoot Fe, Zn, Cu and P concentrations. 3. The eto1 phenotype was rescued toward WT by inoculation with ACC deaminase-producing bacteria, indicating network-level equivalence between plant and microbial interventions.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ACS5; bacterial AcdS module (functional interaction through shared ACC pool)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting ETO1 or its orthologs may increase nutritional value by elevating ethylene-driven micronutrient accumulation, but microbial context should be considered because associated ACC deaminase producers can suppress the phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study uses an eto1 knockout mutant; although the text later mentions ETO1 overexpression once, the experimental evidence concerns loss of ETO1 function increasing ethylene
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; nonbacterized and bacterial inoculation treatments
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential effects on shoot dry weight/other ethylene-regulated traits are implied; detailed agronomic penalties were not the focus of the paper
